fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: New Mexico Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in New Mexico — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license new mexico, dental assistant certification new mexico, RDA new mexico, DANB CDA new mexico, dental assistant radiography new mexico

board.name: New Mexico Board of Dental Health Care

license_types.title: Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in New Mexico.

steps.title: Fulfill Eligibility & Examination Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the educational or experience requirements (graduation from an ADA-accredited dental assisting program or 4000 hours of clinical work experience), possess current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification, pass a Dental Assisting National Board (DANB) exam (General Chairside Assisting, Certified Dental Assistant, or Certified Orthodontic Assistant), and pass the New Mexico Dental Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Register for an Online Account
steps.description: Create a new user account on the New Mexico Medical Board & Affiliated Boards Portal at licensing.rld.nm.gov to access the application system.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log into your account, select the 'Board of Dental Health Care,' then choose 'Registered Dental Assistant,' and begin the 'Initial Application' process within the portal.

steps.title: Upload Required Documentation
steps.description: Submit all necessary supporting documents, such as proof of education/experience, BLS certification, DANB exam scores, and New Mexico Jurisprudence exam results, directly through the online application portal.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $150.00 as prompted within the New Mexico Medical Board & Affiliated Boards Portal.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the New Mexico Board of Dental Health Care by registration.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, New Mexico requires Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in New Mexico.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in New Mexico?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the New Mexico Board of Dental Health Care by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in New Mexico
hero.dek: To become a Registered Dental Assistant in New Mexico, applicants must apply online through the New Mexico Medical Board & Affiliated Boards Portal.

official_links.portal_name: New Mexico Medical Board & Affiliated Boards Portal
official_links.registration_steps: To begin your application, navigate to the portal URL, click 'Register for an Account' if you are a new user, or 'Login' if you have an existing account. Once logged in, select the 'Board of Dental Health Care' and choose the 'Registered Dental Assistant' license type to start the 'Initial Application' process.
